name: Archbee
tagline: AI-powered developer documentation platform for product teams
description: Archbee is a documentation platform built for developer teams, combining AI writing assistance, API references, and knowledge bases in one tool. Its AI co-writer helps draft and update technical docs, while rich blocks support code, API specs, and diagrams natively.
choose_if: building developer-facing documentation with API references
want AI writing assistance built into your doc platform
need a modern alternative to Confluence for technical teams

name: Tettra
tagline: AI-powered internal knowledge base that answers questions from your docs
description: Tettra is an internal knowledge management platform with an AI assistant that answers team questions by searching your documentation. It integrates with Slack for Q&A, automatically suggests when docs are out of date, and routes unanswered questions to the right expert.
choose_if: building an internal knowledge base for an engineering team
want Slack-native AI Q&A over your documentation
tracking documentation health and keeping it current

name: Coda AI
tagline: Doc-meets-spreadsheet platform with AI built directly into every page
description: Coda AI integrates AI writing, summarization, and data manipulation directly into Coda's doc-spreadsheet hybrid. Teams use it to draft PRDs, summarize meeting notes, generate tables from data, and automate repetitive documentation tasks — all within a collaborative workspace.
choose_if: using Coda for product documentation and wikis
want AI writing assistance in a flexible doc-database hybrid
need to automate table generation and data summaries in docs

name: Confluence AI
tagline: Atlassian's Confluence with AI writing and search built in
description: Confluence AI adds Atlassian Intelligence capabilities to Confluence, enabling AI-powered page creation, automatic summaries, smart search, and writing assistance across your team's wiki. It integrates with Jira AI for seamless project-to-doc workflows.
choose_if: already using Atlassian tools and want AI in your wiki
need AI search across a large Confluence knowledge base
building Jira and Confluence workflow automation

name: Productboard AI
tagline: AI-powered product management with feedback synthesis and roadmapping
description: Productboard's AI features automatically synthesize customer feedback from multiple sources, surface themes and insights, and link insights to features. Its AI-assisted prioritization helps product teams make data-driven decisions about what to build next.
choose_if: synthesizing customer feedback at scale to inform product decisions
managing a product roadmap with stakeholder visibility
linking user insights directly to features in a structured PM workflow

name: Jira AI
tagline: Atlassian Intelligence in Jira — AI backlog management and sprint planning
description: Jira AI brings Atlassian Intelligence to issue tracking, enabling AI-generated ticket summaries, automatic subtask creation, sprint retrospective insights, and work-breakdown suggestions. It connects with Confluence AI for end-to-end project documentation and planning.
choose_if: already on Jira and want AI-assisted ticket management
need automatic sprint planning and backlog grooming
scaling a team where Atlassian is the standard tooling

name: Aha! Roadmaps
tagline: AI-powered product roadmapping and strategy platform
description: Aha! is a comprehensive product management platform with AI features for writing product strategy, generating ideas, summarizing feedback, and building visual roadmaps. Its Ideas portal aggregates user feedback and AI helps surface patterns and priorities.
choose_if: building a comprehensive product strategy and roadmap in one tool
need a dedicated ideas portal for collecting user feedback
want AI-assisted goal setting and OKR alignment

name: Grain
tagline: AI meeting notes that turn user interviews into product insights
description: Grain automatically records, transcribes, and summarizes product meetings and user interviews, extracting key insights and action items. Product teams use it to capture user research, share clips, and build an evidence base that links customer voice to product decisions.
choose_if: conducting user interviews and want AI to extract product insights
sharing meeting clips and highlights with stakeholders
building a library of customer evidence for roadmap decisions

name: Google Gemini API
tagline: Google's frontier multimodal model API — Gemini Pro and Flash
description: Google's Gemini API provides access to Gemini Pro, Flash, and Ultra — Google's state-of-the-art multimodal models with native image, audio, and video understanding. With a 1M+ token context window and competitive pricing, it's a strong alternative to GPT-4 for production workloads.
choose_if: need 1M+ token context window for long documents or codebases
want multimodal AI that natively processes images, audio, and video
looking for a cost-competitive frontier model alternative to GPT-4o

name: Amazon Bedrock
tagline: AWS managed AI service with access to frontier models from every provider
description: Amazon Bedrock provides API access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, and Amazon's own Titan models — all through a single AWS API. It handles compliance, security, and private deployment within your AWS account for enterprise-grade AI applications.
choose_if: deploying AI within AWS with existing cloud infrastructure
need multi-model access with enterprise security and compliance
want private model deployment with no data leaving your AWS account

name: Azure OpenAI
tagline: OpenAI models hosted on Azure with enterprise compliance and SLAs
description: Azure OpenAI Service provides access to GPT-4o, o1, and other OpenAI models hosted within Microsoft Azure, offering enterprise-grade SLAs, data privacy guarantees, and compliance certifications. It's the default choice for enterprises on Azure that need OpenAI models.
choose_if: enterprise on Azure with compliance and data residency requirements
need GPT-4o with Azure AD auth and VNet integration
want OpenAI models with enterprise SLAs and support

name: xAI Grok API
tagline: xAI's Grok models with real-time knowledge and strong reasoning
description: xAI's Grok API provides access to the Grok family of frontier language models, featuring competitive reasoning capabilities and an OpenAI-compatible API. Grok-2 and Grok Vision models are available with real-time knowledge and strong coding performance.
choose_if: comparing frontier models beyond OpenAI and Anthropic
want OpenAI-compatible API with Grok's reasoning capabilities
need a model with real-time knowledge cutoff

name: DeepSeek API
tagline: High-performance frontier model API at a fraction of the cost
description: DeepSeek's API provides access to DeepSeek-R1 and DeepSeek-V3 — frontier models matching GPT-4 performance on benchmarks at dramatically lower per-token costs. Its reasoning model (R1) supports extended chain-of-thought for complex coding and math tasks.
choose_if: need frontier-level reasoning at the lowest cost per token
running high-volume inference where cost is the primary constraint
want strong coding and math reasoning at low cost

name: Kilo Code
tagline: Open-source VS Code coding agent with 1.5M users
description: Open-source VS Code extension and superset of Cline + RooCode. Features Architect, Coder, and Debugger modes with a built-in MCP marketplace. Processes 25T tokens in production and ranks #1 coding agent on OpenRouter.
choose_if: You want an open-source VS Code agent with structured Architect/Coder/Debugger modes
You want a built-in MCP marketplace inside your coding agent
You're looking for a battle-tested Cline alternative with 1.5M users

name: RooCode
tagline: Cline fork with intelligent context condensing and custom modes
description: VS Code coding agent forked from Cline with intelligent context window management and highly customizable agent modes. Popular with developers who need reliable multi-file refactoring on large codebases.
choose_if: You need an agent that handles large multi-file changes without losing context
You want a customizable VS Code agent you can fork and modify
You're looking for a lighter alternative to Cline with better context management

name: Warp
tagline: GPU-accelerated terminal with built-in AI agent capabilities
description: Rust-built terminal replacement with native AI agent capabilities. Runs Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI simultaneously in one interface. Pioneering the 'agentic terminal' category — distinct from editor-based AI assistants.
choose_if: You want a terminal that natively integrates multiple AI coding agents
You prefer a GPU-accelerated, modern terminal over VS Code extensions
You want to run Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI side by side

name: OpenAI Agents SDK
tagline: Production-grade multi-agent orchestration from OpenAI
description: OpenAI's production-grade agent framework, successor to Swarm. Core abstraction is explicit handoffs between agents carrying full context. Built for reliable multi-agent pipelines with native OpenAI tool use, tracing, and guardrails.
choose_if: You're building multi-agent systems on top of OpenAI models
You want explicit, auditable agent handoffs with full context passing
You need a production-hardened framework with built-in tracing and guardrails

name: Manus AI
tagline: General-purpose autonomous agent for complex multi-step tasks
description: General-purpose autonomous agent capable of executing complex, multi-step tasks across browser, code, and system interfaces. One of the most widely discussed agentic AI products of 2025, with significant adoption particularly in Asia.
choose_if: You need an agent that handles broad, general-purpose tasks beyond just coding
You want autonomous execution across browser, files, and code in one agent
You're evaluating general autonomous agents alongside coding-specific ones

name: MCP.so
tagline: The largest registry for discovering MCP servers
description: De facto discovery layer for the MCP ecosystem. Indexes tens of thousands of community-built MCP servers with search, filtering, and installation instructions. The go-to starting point when looking for MCP servers for any tool or service.
choose_if: You want to browse and discover MCP servers for your AI setup
You're looking for a specific MCP server for a tool or service
You want to publish your own MCP server for others to find

name: RunPod
tagline: Serverless GPU cloud for AI inference and training
description: On-demand serverless GPU cloud (A100, H100, RTX series) with autoscaling and per-second billing. The go-to choice for indie AI developers and teams that need GPU compute without committing to AWS or GCP reserved instances.
choose_if: You need GPU compute on demand without long-term cloud commitments
You're self-hosting open-source models and need A100/H100 access
You want per-second billing and autoscaling for bursty AI workloads

name: Fal.ai
tagline: Fast serverless inference API for image, video, and audio models
description: Developer API platform for running image, video, and audio generation models (Flux, SDXL, Whisper, and more) at low latency. Popular as a serverless GPU layer for multimodal AI apps, with a clean Python/JS SDK and pay-per-use pricing.
choose_if: You're building multimodal apps that generate images, video, or audio
You want the fastest inference for Flux or SDXL without managing GPUs
You need a serverless alternative to Replicate with a cleaner SDK

name: Maxim AI
tagline: Agent-specific testing and observability platform
description: Testing and observability platform designed specifically for AI agents rather than single LLM calls. Covers simulation testing, multi-turn conversation evaluation, and production monitoring for complex agentic systems.
choose_if: You're building multi-step agents and need more than LLM-level tracing
You want to run simulation tests on agent behavior before deploying
You need observability designed for agentic systems, not just LLM calls

name: Galileo
tagline: Real-time LLM evaluation with sub-200ms guardrail models
description: LLM evaluation platform with evaluation models that run in under 200ms — fast enough to use as production guardrails, not just offline eval. Covers hallucination detection, RAG quality, and safety scoring. Distinct from Galileo AI (the UI design tool).
choose_if: You need real-time LLM guardrails in your production pipeline
You want eval models fast enough (<200ms) to run inline with inference
You need hallucination and RAG quality scoring at production latency

name: Axolotl
tagline: Streamlined LoRA & QLoRA fine-tuning
description: OSS fine-tuning framework built on HuggingFace Transformers. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tuning, and FSDP. Config-driven — define your training run in a YAML file.
choose_if: You want a config-driven OSS fine-tuning pipeline
You need support for LoRA, QLoRA, and FSDP in one tool
You prefer HuggingFace-native workflows

name: Unsloth
tagline: 2× faster, 70% less memory LoRA fine-tuning
description: Dramatically speeds up LoRA and QLoRA fine-tuning by rewriting GPU kernels. Compatible with HuggingFace and works with Llama, Mistral, Gemma, and more. No accuracy loss.
choose_if: You want the fastest OSS LoRA fine-tuning with minimal GPU memory
You're fine-tuning Llama, Mistral, or Gemma models
Memory constraints are the bottleneck in your training setup

name: LlamaFactory
tagline: Unified fine-tuning for 100+ LLMs
description: Supports full fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, DPO, RLHF, and reward modeling across 100+ models. Web UI (LlamaBoard) for no-code training. The most feature-complete OSS fine-tuning framework.
choose_if: You need DPO, RLHF, or reward modeling in addition to SFT
You want a no-code web UI for training runs
You're working across many different model families

name: Torchtune
tagline: PyTorch-native LLM fine-tuning from Meta
description: Meta's official fine-tuning library. Pure PyTorch — no abstraction layers. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, and full fine-tuning for Llama models. Designed for reproducibility and research.
choose_if: You want pure PyTorch with no abstraction layers over training
You're primarily working with Meta's Llama models
Reproducibility and research clarity are priorities

name: Predibase
tagline: Managed fine-tuning and serving for LoRA adapters
description: Commercial platform for fine-tuning and serving open-source LLMs. Specializes in LoRA adapter training with serverless serving. Built by the creators of Ludwig and LoRAX.
choose_if: You want managed fine-tuning without running your own GPU infrastructure
You need to serve many LoRA adapters efficiently on shared base models
You're moving from experimentation to production fine-tuning

name: ElevenLabs
tagline: Ultra-realistic text-to-speech and voice cloning
description: State-of-the-art TTS API with voice cloning, multilingual support, and low-latency streaming. Used in podcasts, audiobooks, conversational AI agents, and game NPCs.
choose_if: You need the most realistic TTS for user-facing applications
You want voice cloning from audio samples
You're building multilingual voice agents

name: Deepgram
tagline: Fast, accurate speech-to-text API
description: Real-time and batch speech recognition API with <300ms latency. Supports 30+ languages, speaker diarization, and custom vocabulary. Nova-3 model is best-in-class for English accuracy.
choose_if: You need real-time speech-to-text with <300ms latency
Accuracy on noisy audio or accented speech matters
You need speaker diarization or custom vocabulary

name: AssemblyAI
tagline: Audio intelligence API — transcription + LLM understanding
description: Transcription API with built-in AI features: speaker labels, sentiment analysis, topic detection, PII redaction, and LeMUR (LLM over audio). Best for extracting structured insight from audio.
choose_if: You need structured intelligence from audio beyond raw transcription
You want LLM-powered Q&A over audio content
PII redaction or compliance on audio data is required

name: Cartesia
tagline: Real-time TTS optimized for conversational AI
description: Ultra-low-latency streaming TTS (<80ms) built for real-time voice agents and phone systems. State Space Model architecture (Sonic) delivers natural prosody at production latency.
choose_if: You're building real-time voice agents where latency is critical (<80ms)
You need streaming TTS that works well in phone systems
You want SSM-based TTS as an alternative to diffusion models

name: PlayHT
tagline: TTS with instant voice cloning
description: Text-to-speech API with instant voice cloning from a 3-second audio sample. Supports 900+ voices, 142 languages, and real-time streaming. Popular for content creation and audiobooks.
choose_if: You need voice cloning from minimal audio samples
You're building multilingual TTS across 100+ languages
Content generation (audiobooks, podcasts) is the primary use case

name: Moondream
tagline: Tiny OSS vision language model
description: 2B parameter vision-language model optimized to run on edge devices and single GPUs. Supports image captioning, visual QA, and object detection. Runs via Ollama or directly with Python.
choose_if: You need a vision model that runs on a single GPU or edge device
You want a compact model for image captioning and visual QA
Low memory footprint is a hard constraint

name: LLaVA
tagline: Open-source multimodal LLM assistant
description: Large Language and Vision Assistant — connects a vision encoder to an LLM for instruction-following with images. OSS research model widely used as a multimodal base. Runs via Ollama.
choose_if: You want an open-source multimodal model for self-hosted deployment
You're doing research on vision-language instruction following
You need a well-documented baseline for multimodal tasks

name: PaliGemma
tagline: Google's OSS vision-language model
description: Google's open-source multimodal model combining SigLIP vision encoder with Gemma LLM. Strong at document understanding, OCR, image captioning, and visual QA. Available via HuggingFace.
choose_if: You need strong OCR and document understanding capabilities
You prefer Google's model family and research provenance
You want a well-maintained open-weight model from a major lab

name: Pixtral
tagline: Mistral's multimodal vision model
description: Mistral's vision-language model available via Mistral API and as open weights. Supports multiple images per prompt, high-resolution understanding, and code extraction from screenshots.
choose_if: You want a commercial vision model with competitive pricing
You need multi-image understanding in a single prompt
You're already using Mistral's API ecosystem

name: Qwen-VL
tagline: Alibaba's open-weight vision-language model
description: Qwen Visual Language model series from Alibaba. Strong at multilingual visual understanding, document parsing, and chart reading. Available as open weights on HuggingFace. Runs via vLLM.
choose_if: You need multilingual visual understanding (especially CJK languages)
Chart, table, and document parsing is the primary use case
You want strong performance across multiple model sizes

name: InternVL2
tagline: Top OSS multimodal model from OpenGVLab
description: InternVL2 series from Shanghai AI Lab — consistently top-ranked on open-source multimodal benchmarks. Strong at document understanding, chart analysis, and multi-image reasoning.
choose_if: You want the highest benchmark scores among open-source vision models
Multi-image and high-resolution document understanding is required
You're comparing models and want the strongest open-weight option

name: Vapi
tagline: Voice agent middleware for production phone and web agents
description: Full-stack platform for building, testing, and deploying voice AI agents. Handles real-time telephony, multi-provider LLM routing, interruption detection, and call analytics. Most-adopted voice agent infrastructure in production.
choose_if: You're building phone or web voice agents with real-time conversation
You need multi-provider LLM + TTS + STT routing in one platform
You want production-grade call analytics and interruption handling

name: Retell AI
tagline: Low-latency conversational voice AI platform
description: Platform for building production voice agents with sub-800ms latency end-to-end. Handles telephony, turn-taking, backchannels, and interruptions natively. Strong on call reliability and HIPAA compliance for healthcare use cases.
choose_if: Latency is critical — sub-800ms end-to-end response is required
You're building healthcare or compliance-sensitive voice agents (HIPAA)
You want native backchannels and human-like turn-taking out of the box

name: Browser Use
tagline: OSS AI agent library for browser control
description: Python library that gives AI agents full browser control — navigate, click, fill forms, extract data. Built on Playwright. Reached 50K GitHub stars faster than most AI infra projects. Works with any LLM.
choose_if: You need AI agents to control a browser and interact with any website
You want OSS browser automation that works with your existing LLM
Tasks involve form filling, navigation, or multi-step web workflows

name: Stagehand
tagline: AI-native browser automation framework by Browserbase
description: TypeScript framework for AI browser automation built on Playwright. Introduces act/extract/observe primitives that let LLMs control browsers with natural language. Action caching (v3) makes repeated flows deterministic and cheap.
choose_if: You're building in TypeScript and want idiomatic browser automation
You need action caching to make LLM-driven flows deterministic
You want Browserbase's managed browser infrastructure under the hood

name: Skyvern
tagline: Vision-based browser automation for complex web tasks
description: Uses vision + LLMs to navigate websites without fragile CSS selectors. Specializes in complex portals, insurance forms, and government sites that break traditional automation. Self-hosted or managed cloud.
choose_if: You're automating complex portals or forms that break selector-based tools
Vision-based navigation is needed for dynamic or legacy web UIs
You want self-hostable browser automation with a workflow engine

name: Firecrawl
tagline: Turn any website into clean data for AI
description: Crawl and scrape websites into clean markdown or structured JSON — handles JavaScript rendering, PDFs, and dynamic content. LLM-ready output. Used as the data-ingestion layer for RAG pipelines and AI research agents.
choose_if: You need to turn websites into clean markdown for RAG pipelines
Your agent needs to research the web and extract structured data
You're building a crawler that handles JS-rendered and dynamic sites
